projects.name: Aleph
projects.desc: One place for US importers to handle product compliance — CPSIA, Prop 65, PFAS, and FSVP.
projects.next_up: Get the first group of companies using it before we start charging.
roadmap_items: Welcoming the first companies onto the platform
Letting each company adjust which rules apply to them
Expanding into more product categories

projects.name: The Fantastic Leagues
projects.desc: AI-powered fantasy baseball for serious keeper leagues — running live for the OGBA league this season.
projects.next_up: Make the standings and daily stat lines more accurate during the season.
roadmap_items: More accurate standings
Pull in player projections from FanGraphs
Add football and basketball

projects.name: Bahtzang Trader
projects.desc: A trading experiment — Claude makes buy, sell, and hold calls using a practice account with no real money.
projects.next_up: Six more practice trades before deciding whether to trade real money.
roadmap_items: Finish the current round of practice trades
Decide whether to trade with real money
Tighten the rules that limit losses

projects.name: The Judge Tool
projects.desc: Digital scoring for KCBS barbecue competitions — replaces clipboards and carbon-copy score sheets.
projects.next_up: Setting up time with organizers to test the product and confirm it's worth building out.
roadmap_items: A round of testing with real organizers
A clearer screen for totaling scores
Using it at live competitions

projects.name: TableDrop
projects.desc: A marketplace for hard-to-get Taipei restaurant reservations.
projects.next_up: Get one reservation working end to end, from listing to purchase.
roadmap_items: Make the full booking process reliable
Handing off the reservation to the buyer
Signing up more restaurants

projects.name: Tastemakers
projects.desc: A social app for finding places to eat. The server is running; the phone apps are paused.
projects.next_up: Move the database to a more reliable host.
roadmap_items: Move the database to a new host
Rebuild the iPhone app
More ways to share recommendations with friends

projects.name: The Thirsty Pig
projects.desc: My food blog from 2007 to today — 1,639 posts and more than 1,400 mapped LA restaurants.
projects.next_up: Finish tagging every restaurant, then launch the new design.
roadmap_items: Tagging every restaurant on the site
A bold new look for the blog
Sorting through eighteen years of photos

projects.name: KTV Singer
projects.desc: A home karaoke system — everyone's phone becomes a remote for the Apple TV, and the song list comes from YouTube.
projects.next_up: Make it reliable enough that friends can use it without my help.
roadmap_items: Connecting phones to the TV without fuss
Easier song search
Keeping everyone's phone in sync

projects.name: jameschang.co
projects.desc: This site — hand-built with no frameworks, and it updates itself from eight live data feeds.
projects.next_up: Keep adding to it, and keep the résumé PDF current.
roadmap_items: A better-looking résumé PDF
More live data feeds
Fuller write-ups of each project

projects.name: Vouch
projects.desc: A platform where students share the change they want to make, mentors vouch for them, and supporters back them — built to keep minors safe, moderated by default.
projects.next_up: Set up real parent accounts and turn on live donations.
roadmap_items: Parent accounts and student profiles
Real donations, not test payments
Open it up to real students

projects.name: Spar
projects.desc: A voice app where leaders rehearse difficult conversations against an AI counterpart, then get a coached debrief on how they handled it.
projects.next_up: Make sure the conversation feels real before building anything around it.
roadmap_items: Hold the conversation inside the app itself
The coaching feedback afterward — the real product
A library of situations to practice, set up by voice
